title: Explain Inputfeed To Text
short_answer: inputfeed_to_text is the app block that turns live audio into text.
text: inputfeed_to_text is like the app's ear. It listens to audio, sends speech through STT, and writes transcript files for other app parts to read.
more: The module lives in app/inputfeed_to_text. The main service is app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_to_text_service.py, and it writes through transcript_runtime.py into runtime/sacred and runtime/status. This block does not decide commands, memory, persona, HELP, router, or TTS; it only produces text.

title: Explain Audio To Text
short_answer: Audio to text is the flow that captures microphone audio and turns it into transcript records.
text: Audio to text starts with the mic, reads audio in small chunks, filters it if enabled, and sends it to STT. The result is written as transcript text.
more: The README flow is microphone -> mic_audio_source.py -> optional RNNoise -> optional Silero VAD -> Moonshine or Whisper -> transcript_runtime.py -> runtime/sacred files. If the app hears nothing, check WINDOWS_MIC_DEVICE_NAME, INPUTFEED_SOURCE_BACKEND, and runtime/status/inputfeed_to_text_status.json first.

title: Explain Live Transcript
short_answer: Live transcript is the current speech text written by inputfeed_to_text.
text: Live transcript files are the text output from the listener. Other app parts can read them instead of talking directly to the microphone.
more: Raw live transcript files are runtime/sacred/live_transcript_raw.jsonl and runtime/sacred/live_transcript_raw_latest.json. Compatibility names also exist as runtime/sacred/live_transcript_history.jsonl and runtime/sacred/live_transcript_latest.json. These are written by transcript_runtime.py.

title: Explain Raw Transcript
short_answer: Raw transcript is each direct STT text event before phrase assembly.
text: Raw transcript keeps the short pieces that come straight from STT. Use it when you need to see exactly what the listener wrote first.
more: Raw records are appended by append_raw_transcript_line(...) in app/inputfeed_to_text/transcript_runtime.py. They include event_id, ts, session_id, source, text, language, and metadata such as stt_backend and model_name. Check runtime/sacred/live_transcript_raw.jsonl for the full stream.

title: Explain Assembled Transcript
short_answer: Assembled transcript joins nearby raw transcript pieces into more useful phrases.
text: Assembled transcript is cleaner reading text made from small raw transcript parts. It helps turn chopped speech into fuller phrases.
more: The builder lives in app/inputfeed_to_text/assembled_transcript_builder.py. It reads runtime/sacred/live_transcript_raw.jsonl, merges nearby records using ASSEMBLER_MERGE_WINDOW_SECONDS, flushes after ASSEMBLER_FLUSH_IDLE_SECONDS, and writes runtime/sacred/live_transcript_assembled.jsonl plus live_transcript_assembled_latest.json.

title: Explain Transcript Runtime
short_answer: Transcript runtime creates folders and writes transcript, status, and archive files.
text: Transcript runtime is the file-writing layer for inputfeed_to_text. It keeps live transcript files, latest JSON files, status files, and session archives organized.
more: The owner file is app/inputfeed_to_text/transcript_runtime.py. It ensures runtime/sacred, runtime/status, and runtime/stt_archive exist; writes raw and assembled transcript records; writes inputfeed and assembler status; and archives sessions under runtime/stt_archive/YYYY/MM/DD when services stop.

title: Explain Inputfeed Settings
short_answer: Inputfeed settings are ENV-backed values for audio capture, STT, filtering, streaming, and assembly.
text: Inputfeed settings control how the listener captures audio and writes text. Use them when tuning mic behavior, STT backend, VAD, streaming, or transcript assembly.
more: The main settings file is app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py. It defines runtime paths, source backend, sample rate, input gain, STT backend, model, language, VAD settings, RNNoise switch, streaming settings, assembler settings, and transcript source/session fields. source_config.py exports resolved values for the service.

title: Explain Inputfeed ENV
short_answer: Inputfeed ENV values change listener behavior without editing code.
text: Inputfeed ENV is how you tune this listener from outside the Python files. Use it for mic backend, STT backend, model, language, VAD, streaming, and assembler values.
more: Important ENV names are defined in app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py and listed in the README. Examples include INPUTFEED_SOURCE_BACKEND, WINDOWS_MIC_DEVICE_NAME, INPUTFEED_STT_BACKEND, MOONSHINE_LANGUAGE, STT_MODEL, STT_LANGUAGE, INPUTFEED_ENABLE_RNNOISE, INPUTFEED_ENABLE_SILERO_VAD, INPUTFEED_USE_STREAMING, and ASSEMBLER_MERGE_WINDOW_SECONDS. Only change values you understand because they affect live listening quality.

title: Explain Inputfeed Status
short_answer: Inputfeed status shows whether the listener or assembler is starting, running, stopped, or errored.
text: Status files are the first place to check when listening is broken. They show the current state and useful last-event details.
more: The listener writes runtime/status/inputfeed_to_text_status.json through write_inputfeed_status(...). The assembler writes runtime/status/assembled_transcript_builder_status.json through write_assembled_status(...). If words are missing or cut off, inspect status fields like last_event, stt_backend, last_text, last_rms, gate_backend, and streaming_enabled.

title: Explain STT Providers
short_answer: STT providers are the backends that turn audio into text.
text: The main inputfeed overview can point you to STT providers, but backend details live in providers_help.json. Moonshine and Whisper are provider topics.
more: Provider selection is controlled by INPUTFEED_STT_BACKEND in app/inputfeed_to_text/inputfeed_settings.py. The service branches to Moonshine when the backend is moonshine, otherwise it uses faster-whisper through the Whisper path. See providers_help.json in app/system_support/HELP/APP/INPUTFEED_TO_TEXT for Moonshine, Whisper, faster-whisper, provider backend, and base provider details.
